title: Malty Springy Mexican Fish Tacos
description: A comforting, pescatarian-friendly Mexican dish featuring ice-bath-shocked cod, malty flavor, and a crunchy slaw made with paprika-spiced cabbage.
instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or aluminum foil.
Place the cod fillets on the prepared baking sheet and season with salt, pepper, cumin, coriander, and paprika.
Drizzle olive oil over the fillets and gently rub it in with your fingers.
Bake the cod in the preheated oven for 8-10 minutes or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
Remove the cod from the oven and carefully place it in an ice bath to shock and stop the cooking process.
While the cod is cooling, mix together the shredded cabbage, diced tomatoes, and a squeeze of lime juice in a bowl.
Once the cod has cooled, break it into large chunks and add it to the cabbage mixture.
Stir in the sour cream and mash the avocado with a fork to create a chunky slaw.
Warm some tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the fish mixture onto a warmed tortilla and topping with the slaw.
Finish with a sprinkle of salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lime juice.
